title: The Passport (Amendment) Rules, 1993 (10.07.1993)
text: REGD. NO. D.L.-33004/93 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Sub-section (i) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY Me. 235] NEW DELHI, SATURDAY, JULY 10,1993/ASADHA 19, 1915 Separate Paging is given to this Part in order that it may be filed as a separate compilation 1542 GI/93 (1) -- 1 of 8 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: FXTRAORD1NARY [PART n~Sfic. ^ i > ] -- 2 of 8 -- 3 -- 3 of 8 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(J)J -- 4 of 8 -- 5 MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 10th Inly, 1993 G. R. 5O31E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by section 24, read with section 5 of the Passport Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), «ie Central Government hereby make the following rules further to amend the Passport Rules, 1980, namely: (1) These rules may be called the Passport (Amendment) Rules* 1993. (2) They shall come into force with effect from 10 July, 1993 2. For Schedule IV to the Passport Rules, 1980, the following shall be substituted, namely:- SCHEDULE IV (See rule 8) SCHEDULE OF FEES PAYABLE IN RESPECT OF APPLICATIONS FOR PASSPORT AND TRAVBL DOCUMENTS SI. No 1 Particulars of application * — 2 NEW PASSPORTS 1. 2. 3, Ordinary passport with a maximum validity of ten years (no extra fee will be charged for rene- wals up to ten years if initial validity is less) India-Bangladesh Passport with a maximum initial validity of three years (no extra foe will be charged for renewals upto three years if the initial validity is less) India-Sri Lanka Passport with a maximum initial validity of four years (no extra fees will be charged for renewals upto four years life if initial validity is less). SPECIAL TRAVEL DOCUMENTS- 4. 5. 6. Emergency Certificate Certificate of identity with a maximum initial validity of six years. Additional passport for any country under the proviso to rule 13 upto initial validity of one year (no extra fee will be charged for renewals upto ten years). RENEWAL OF TRAVEL DOCUMENTS— 7, 8. 9. Renewal of ordinary passport with an initial validity of five years for 3 maximum validity of ten years (the renewal fee will be charged in one lump sum once only even though period of validity may be less than five years depending upon discretion of Passport Issuing Authority or according to instructions from Government authorities). Renewal of India-Bangladesh Passport with a maximum validity of three years after initial validity of three years (the renewal fee will be charged in one lump sum once only, even though period of validity may be less than three years, depending upon discretion of Passport Issuing Authority or according to instructions from Government authorities). -Renewal of India-Sri Lanka passport with a maximum validity of four years after initial vali- dity of four years (the renewal fee will be charged in one lump sum once only, even though period of validity may be less than four years depending upon discretion of Passport Issuing Authority or according to instructions from Government authorities).
